findings.description: CI security review flagged TracingManifestConfig.endpoint and LineageManifestConfig.endpoint as SSRF vectors (CWE-918). Pydantic str fields accepted arbitrary URLs including ftp://, file:///etc/passwd, and AWS metadata endpoint. Gate-security missed this — it focuses on code patterns, not attack surface.
findings.proposedRule: Pydantic models with endpoint/URL fields MUST validate http/https scheme and reject private network targets

findings.description: Bumping COMPILED_ARTIFACTS_VERSION from 0.7.0 to 0.8.0 required regenerating all 8 OCI golden fixture files. Not planned as a task — consumed 2 fix-up commits.
findings.proposedRule: Version bumps must include golden fixture regeneration as an explicit subtask

findings.description: Regenerated golden fixtures contained hex checksums that triggered detect-secrets false positives. First git push failed on pre-push hook. Required .secrets.baseline update.
findings.proposedRule: After modifying golden fixtures, run detect-secrets scan --baseline before pushing

findings.description: Gate-security found 0 BLOCK/0 WARN for WU-9. CI security review found 1 CRITICAL + 2 HIGH + 4 MEDIUM on the same code. They catch fundamentally different classes of issues.
findings.proposedRule: Gate-security and CI security review are complementary layers, not redundant — both must be green

findings.description: WU-9 produced 12 commits: 7 task + 5 fix-up. Fix-ups were all predictable downstream effects of schema changes (golden fixtures, secrets baseline, exports, security). Recurring pattern from WU-6 and WU-8.
findings.proposedRule: Schema change work units must include a ripple checklist covering re-exports, golden fixtures, secrets baseline, and contract test updates
